name: Londres
desc: Lo básico: verbos, plurales y vocabulario cotidiano.

questions.q: What is the opposite of 'big'?
questions.explain: Big (grande) ↔ Small (pequeño). Tall es alto, long es largo y fast es rápido.

questions.q: She _______ coffee every morning.
questions.explain: Con he/she/it en presente simple, el verbo lleva -s: she drinks, he works, it rains.

questions.q: Which word means 'viaje'?
questions.explain: Trip = viaje. Cuidado con los parecidos: trap (trampa), tape (cinta), type (tipo/escribir).

questions.q: _______ are you from?
questions.explain: Where = dónde (lugar). What = qué, who = quién, when = cuándo.

questions.q: There _______ two airports in this city.
questions.explain: There are + plural (two airports). There is + singular (one airport).

questions.q: You use a _______ to travel to another country.
questions.explain: Passport = pasaporte. Password es contraseña — ¡no los confundas en el aeropuerto! 😄

questions.q: "The museum is open from 9 AM to 5 PM, Monday to Saturday." — Can you visit on Sunday?
questions.explain: Dice 'Monday to Saturday' (lunes a sábado), así que el domingo está cerrado (closed).

questions.q: I _______ to the gym yesterday.
questions.explain: 'Yesterday' indica pasado → went (pasado irregular de go).

questions.q: What is the opposite of 'hot'?
questions.explain: Hot (caliente) ↔ Cold (frío). Warm es tibio, wet es mojado.

questions.q: What's the plural of 'child'?
questions.explain: Child es irregular: su plural es children, no lleva -s como los sustantivos regulares.

questions.q: Which word means 'equipaje'?
questions.explain: Luggage = equipaje. Language es idioma, lounge es sala de espera.

questions.q: They _______ from Spain.
questions.explain: They (plural) + are. He/she/it usa is, I usa am.

questions.q: I usually _______ breakfast at 7 AM.
questions.explain: Con I/you/we/they en presente simple se usa la forma base del verbo: have.

questions.q: "Flight AA202 to London has been delayed by two hours." — What happened to the flight?
questions.explain: Delayed = retrasado. El vuelo saldrá más tarde de lo previsto, no fue cancelado.

questions.q: What do you call the document you show before boarding a plane?
questions.explain: Boarding pass = pase de abordar. Ticket es más general (boleto).

questions.q: How _______ apples do you want?
questions.explain: Many se usa con sustantivos contables en plural (apples). Much es para incontables.

name: Nueva York
desc: Tiempos verbales, phrasal verbs y comprensión.

questions.q: I have lived here _______ 2020.
questions.explain: Since + punto en el tiempo (since 2020). For + duración (for six years).

questions.q: The company decided to 'put off' the meeting. What does 'put off' mean?
questions.explain: Put off = postpone (posponer). Es uno de los phrasal verbs más comunes en el trabajo.

questions.q: If it rains tomorrow, we _______ at home.
questions.explain: Primer condicional: If + presente simple, will + verbo base. Situación real y futura.

questions.q: "Despite the heavy traffic, Maria arrived on time." — What does this tell us?
options: Maria was late because of traffic
Maria was punctual even with traffic
questions.explain: Despite = a pesar de. Hubo tráfico pesado PERO llegó a tiempo (on time = puntual).

questions.q: She's the person _______ helped me find a job.
questions.explain: Who se usa para personas. Which para cosas, whose para posesión, where para lugares.

questions.q: Which word means 'to improve a skill through repeated exercise'?
questions.explain: To practice = practicar. Exactly what you're doing right now 💪

questions.q: You _______ smoke inside the airport. It's forbidden.
questions.explain: Mustn't = prohibición (no debes). 'Don't have to' significa que no es necesario, ¡es distinto!

questions.q: "The flight was delayed due to bad weather." — Why was the flight late?
questions.explain: Due to = debido a. Bad weather = mal tiempo. 'Delayed' significa retrasado.

questions.q: I _______ never been to Australia before.
questions.explain: Present perfect con I/you/we/they: have + participio (been).

questions.q: The meeting was 'called off' at the last minute. What does 'called off' mean?
questions.explain: Call off = cancel (cancelar). Distinto de 'put off' (postpone/posponer).

questions.q: If I _______ more time, I would travel more often.
questions.explain: Segundo condicional: If + pasado simple (had), would + verbo base. Situación hipotética presente.

questions.q: "Although it was raining, the flight departed on schedule." — What does this tell us?
options: The flight was delayed by rain
The flight left on time despite the rain
questions.explain: Although = aunque. A pesar de la lluvia, el vuelo salió a tiempo (on schedule).

questions.q: This is the hotel _______ we stayed last summer.
questions.explain: Where se usa para lugares (the hotel where...). Who es personas, whose es posesión.

questions.q: Which word means 'to reserve something in advance'?
questions.explain: To book = reservar. Ejemplo: 'I booked a hotel room online.'

questions.q: You _______ arrive early; the gates open at 6 AM sharp.
questions.explain: Should = recomendación (deberías). Aquí se sugiere llegar temprano porque las puertas abren a las 6 AM.

questions.q: "The airline offered a refund because the flight was cancelled." — Why did passengers get their money back?
questions.explain: Refund = reembolso. Se dio porque el vuelo fue cancelado (cancelled), no solo retrasado.

name: Sídney
desc: Condicionales, voz pasiva y vocabulario profesional.

questions.q: If the company _______ usage limits earlier, it wouldn't have burned its budget.
questions.explain: Tercer condicional: If + past perfect (had set), would have + participio. Situación hipotética del pasado. ¿Te suena? 😉

questions.q: The 'rationale' behind a decision is closest in meaning to:
questions.explain: Rationale = la razón o justificación de fondo. Palabra muy frecuente en textos académicos e IELTS.

questions.q: Access to the new model _______ by the government until the framework is finalized.
questions.explain: Voz pasiva en presente continuo: is/are + being + participio. Común en textos técnicos y de noticias.

questions.q: ETFs posted $4.5 billion in net 'outflows'. What are outflows?
questions.explain: Outflows = dinero saliendo (retiros). Lo contrario es inflows (dinero entrando). Vocabulario clave de mercados.

questions.q: "The statement was rare, which underscores the severity of the threat." — 'Underscores' means:
questions.explain: Underscore = subrayar, enfatizar. Sinónimos útiles: highlight, emphasize, stress.

questions.q: By the time we land, the crew _______ three meals.
questions.explain: Future perfect: will have + participio. Acción que estará completada antes de un momento futuro.

questions.q: A 'liability' in a financial context is:
questions.explain: Liability = pasivo, obligación o responsabilidad financiera/legal. Opuesto a asset (activo).

questions.q: Not only _______ the exam, but she also got the highest score.
questions.explain: Inversión con 'Not only': Not only + auxiliar + sujeto + verbo. Estructura avanzada que suma puntos en IELTS writing.

questions.q: "Had the ETF inflows stabilized, analysts would have revised their targets upward." — What actually happened?
options: Inflows stabilized and targets went up
questions.explain: Tercer condicional invertido (Had X happened = If X had happened). Describe algo que NO ocurrió: los flujos no se estabilizaron.

questions.q: Were it not for the strict visa rules, more tourists _______ this country.
questions.explain: 'Were it not for' = 'If it were not for' (si no fuera por). Inversión del segundo condicional: would + verbo base.

questions.q: A 'subsidiary' company is best described as:
options: A company controlled by another (parent) company
questions.explain: Subsidiary = filial, empresa controlada por una matriz (parent company). Vocabulario común en negocios.

questions.q: The proposal, _______ was rejected twice, will be presented again next month.
questions.explain: Which introduce una cláusula no restrictiva sobre cosas (the proposal). 'That' no se usa después de coma en este tipo de cláusula.

questions.q: To 'mitigate' a risk means to:
questions.explain: Mitigate = mitigar, reducir el impacto o severidad de algo. Muy común en informes de riesgo.

questions.q: "Were it not for the analysts' warnings, the fund would have doubled its exposure." — What actually happened?
options: The fund did not double its exposure
questions.explain: La estructura hipotética indica que, gracias a las advertencias, el fondo NO duplicó su exposición.

questions.q: Scarcely _______ the announcement been made when share prices dropped.
questions.explain: Inversión con 'scarcely... when': Scarcely + had + sujeto + participio. Estructura formal avanzada.

questions.q: The report's 'implications' are best understood as its:
questions.explain: Implications = implicaciones, consecuencias posibles de algo. Clave en textos analíticos.

questions.q: The merger, had it been approved, _______ the industry significantly.
questions.explain: Tercer condicional invertido: 'had it been approved' = 'if it had been approved'. Serie: would have + participio.

questions.q: "Not until the audit was completed did the discrepancies come to light." — When were the discrepancies discovered?
questions.explain: 'Not until X did Y' = Y no pasó hasta que X pasó. Las discrepancias se descubrieron después de completar la auditoría (after).
